data.description:
## Problem
tests/unit/test_overlap_retrieve.py::test_perf asserts a fixed 50ms wall-clock budget on a SINGLE run of rebar.llm.overlap.retrieve() over 300 digests (line 63-66). On a contended CI runner the sample inflates far past budget and fails the Verified gate for unrelated changes. Observed: Gerrit run 29964385401 (ubuntu py3.11) failed 'retrieve took 289.5 ms (budget < 50 ms)' while py3.12/py3.13/macos passed in the SAME run.

## Proven root cause (rebar-debug)
Single-sample wall-clock time is not robust to shared-runner CPU scheduling contention. The function itself is fast: measured 1.6ms median / 2.1ms max / 0-of-20-over-budget locally (~30x margin). The 289.5ms CI sample is ~180x the true cost = transient runner contention, not algorithmic cost. Introduced by 6558e2bfa (2026-07-09). Changeable artifact: the single-sample measurement + hardcoded threshold at test_overlap_retrieve.py:66.

## Fix
Measure the MINIMUM elapsed across N=5 runs (best-of-N) and assert min < budget. The min reflects the uncontended compute cost (at least one of N samples hits a quiet slice), so transient contention no longer flakes the gate; a real algorithmic regression slows every run including the min, so the guardrail retains teeth. Keep the 50ms budget (huge margin on the ~1.6ms true cost).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_perf measures best-of-N (min over >=5 runs), asserts min < budget; no single-sample assertion remains.
- [ ] Robustness: a simulated transient stall on one sample no longer fails the assertion (whereas the old single-sample form does).
- [ ] Teeth retained: a sustained/uniform slowdown (real regression) still fails the assertion.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
data.title: Flaky perf gate: test_overlap_retrieve.test_perf single-sample wall-clock budget breaks under CI runner contention
